title: trigger next
text: trigger next Trigger that responds to a request for the next field in a form component. Declaration: trigger next Applies to: Field in form components. Activation: Activated by the structure editor functions ^NEXT_FIELD or ^FIELD (when the direction is Next), after the loseFocus trigger completes successfully. This trigger is not activated when the cursor is moved with arrow keys or mouse clicks, or by setting $prompt . Default behavior: None Behavior upon completion: None If $status is less than zero, the user cannot leave the field. However, the user is free to leave the field in a way that does not activate this trigger, for example, with a structure editor function such as ^PREVIOUS_FIELD or with the cursor keys. Description This trigger can be used to control the prompting sequence of fields. It should not be used for data validation processing because the user can leave the field in a way that does not activate this trigger. For more information, see trigger getFocus (Field) and trigger loseFocus (Field) . Example: next You can use change the default prompting sequence of fields by setting $prompt to the next appropriate field. The following example prompts for a maiden name if the user enters a gender of ‘F’ (for female): trigger next ; Field (of GENDER) if (GENDER = "F") ; female, so prompt for maidenname $prompt = MAIDEN_NAME endif otherwise, use default prompt sequence end; next Copy Related Links trigger getFocus (Field) Trigger that reacts to a field getting focus in a Form component. It is usually used for user interactions such as prompting the user with a hint message, or modifying the way a field is displayed. trigger loseFocus (Field) Trigger that defines what happens after a user successfully leaves a field in a form component. trigger previous Trigger that responds to a request for the previous field in a form component.
